title: Battery architecture in an electronic device
abstract: An electronic device having a battery assembly is disclosed. Unlike traditional battery assemblies that include rectilinear electrodes with two sides of equal length, the battery assemblies described herein may include electrodes having a shape/configuration resembling an L-shape electrode used to form chemical reactions in order to generate electrical energy. However, other shapes/configurations are possible. The shape/configuration of the housing of the battery assembly confirms to the shape/configuration of the electrodes. Further, in order to accommodate an internal component (such as a circuit board assembly), the shape of the battery assembly provides additional space in the electronic device. In order to form the electrodes, the electrodes may undergo a die cutting operation. Also, the housing may include a channel, or reduced dimension, that accommodates a flexible circuit in the electronic device that passes over the battery assembly at the channel.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A battery assembly for an electronic device, the battery assembly comprising: a housing that defines a cavity; and a single piece electrode disposed in the cavity, the single piece electrode having a shape that includes: a first wall characterized as having a first dimension, a second wall characterized as having a second dimension that is different than the first dimension, the second wall being parallel to the first wall, and a third wall that joins together the first wall and the second wall, wherein the housing conforms to the shape of the single piece electrode. 2. The battery assembly of claim 1, wherein: the single piece electrode further comprises a fourth wall extending from one of the first wall and the second wall, the fourth wall being parallel to the third wall, the third wall characterized as having a third dimension, and the fourth wall characterized as having a fourth dimension different than the third dimension. 3. The battery assembly of claim 2, wherein the first wall, the second wall, the third wall, and the fourth wall combine to at least partially define an L-shape. 4. The battery assembly of claim 2, wherein the single piece electrode further comprises a fifth wall characterized as having a fifth dimension, the fifth wall parallel with respect to the third wall and the fourth wall. 5. The battery assembly of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the single piece electrode is a first single piece electrode, and wherein the battery assembly further comprises: a second single piece electrode; and a separator positioned between the first single piece electrode and the second single piece electrode, wherein the second single piece electrode and the separator have a size and shape in accordance with the shape of the first single piece electrode. 6. The battery assembly of claim 5, further comprising a through hole formed in the housing, the through hole defined by: a first opening in the first single piece electrode; 

 a second opening in the second single piece electrode, the second opening aligned with the first opening; and a third opening in the separator, the third opening aligned with the first opening and the second opening. The battery assembly of any one of claims 1-4, further comprising: a channel formed in the housing; and a circuit board positioned in the cavity in a location corresponding to channel. 8 An electronic device, comprising: an enclosure defining an internal volume; a battery assembly disposed in the internal volume, the battery assembly comprising: a first edge, a second edge separate from the first edge, and a channel; a circuit board assembly at least partially positioned between the first edge and the second edge; an internal component; and a flexible circuit electrically coupled to the circuit board assembly, the flexible circuit passing over the battery assembly along the channel and connecting to the internal component such that the internal component is electrically coupled with the circuit board assembly. 9. The electronic device of claim 8, wherein the battery assembly further comprises an electrode that includes: a first rectangular portion symmetrically disposed about a first longitudinal axis; and a second rectangular portion disposed about a second longitudinal axis that is perpendicular with respect to the first longitudinal axis. 10. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the first rectangular portion includes a first wall parallel to the first longitudinal axis, the first wall having a first dimension, and wherein the second rectangular portion includes a second wall parallel to the second longitudinal axis, the second wall having a second dimension less than the first dimension. 11. The electronic device of claim 9, wherein the electrode further comprises a third rectangular portion that is parallel with respect to the second rectangular portion and perpendicular with respect to the first rectangular portion. 12. The electronic device of any one of claims 8-11, wherein the battery assembly further comprises a circuit board positioned in a location corresponding to the channel. 13. The electronic device of any one of claims 8-11, wherein the internal component is a first internal component, and wherein the electronic device further comprises a second internal component disposed within the internal volume, wherein a portion of the battery assembly elevates over the second internal component to cover the second internal component. 14. The electronic device of any one of claims 8-11, wherein the internal component is a first internal component, and wherein the electronic device further comprises a second internal component, wherein the battery assembly includes an opening, and wherein the second internal component is positioned in the opening. 15. The electronic device of any one of claims 8-11, wherein the internal component comprises an audio module. 16. A method for assembling a battery assembly for an electronic device, the method comprising: providing a housing that defines a cavity; cutting a single piece electrode to a shape that includes: a first wall having a first dimension, a second wall having a second dimension that is different than the first dimension, the second wall being parallel to the first wall, and a third wall that joins together the first wall and the second wall; and inserting the single piece electrode into the cavity, wherein the housing conforms to the shape of the single piece electrode. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein providing the housing comprises forming a channel in the housing. 18. The method of any one of claims 16-17, wherein cutting the single piece electrode comprises die cutting the single piece electrode, wherein die cutting the single piece electrode comprises: forming a first rectangular portion; and 

 forming a second rectangular portion that extends from, and is perpendicular to, the first rectangular portion. 19. The method of any one of claims 16-17, further comprising forming a through hole in the battery assembly, the through hole defined by an opening in the housing and an opening in the single piece electrode. 20. The method of any one of claims 16-17, wherein cutting the single piece electrode cutting a first single piece electrode, and wherein the method further comprises: inserting a second single piece electrode into the cavity; and inserting a separator into the cavity between the first single piece electrode and the second single piece electrode, wherein the second single piece electrode and the separator include a size and shape in accordance with the shape of the first single piece electrode. 21. A battery assembly for an electronic device, the battery assembly comprising: a housing that includes a channel defining a reduced dimension in the housing such that the housing includes a first height and a second height, at the channel, that is less than the first height; multiple electrodes disposed in the housing, the multiple electrodes separated by a separator; and a circuit board that monitors electrical current to the multiple electrodes, the circuit board disposed in the housing at a location corresponding to the channel. 22. The battery assembly of claim 21, wherein the housing comprises a first cover element and a second cover element sealed with the first cover element. 23. The battery assembly of claim 22, wherein the housing and each of the multiple electrodes comprises an L-shaped configuration. 24. The battery assembly of claim 22, wherein the L-shaped configuration accommodates a circuit board assembly in the electronic device. 25. The battery assembly of claim 24, wherein in the separator includes a shape that resembles the L-shaped configuration. 26. The battery assembly of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the housing and each of the multiple electrodes comprises an I-shaped configuration. 27. The battery assembly of claim 26, wherein separator includes a shape that resembles the I-shaped configuration. 28. The battery assembly of any one of claims 21-24, wherein the housing comprises an opening designed to receive a component, and wherein each of the multiple electrodes comprises an opening aligned with the opening of the housing. 29. An electronic device, comprising: a metal band that defines sidewall components; a bottom wall coupled with the sidewall components; and a battery assembly comprising an electrode that is cut into a first section and a second section perpendicular to the first section. 30. The electronic device of claim 29, wherein the electrode further comprises a third section perpendicular to the first section and parallel to the second section. 31. The electronic device of claim 29, wherein the electrode resembles an L- shape. 32. The electronic device of claim 29, wherein the electrode resembles an I-shape. 33. The electronic device of claim 29, further comprising a channel in the battery assembly, the channel configured to receive a flexible circuit. 34. The electronic device of claim 33, further comprising a circuit board positioned in the battery assembly in a location corresponding to the channel, the circuit board configured to monitor electrical current to the electrode. 35. The electronic device of any one of claims 29-34, wherein the battery assembly further comprises: a second electrode; and a separator positioned between the electrode and the second electrode, wherein each of the electrode, the second electrode, and the separator include a first rectangular portion and a second rectangular portion perpendicular to the first rectangular portion. 36. The electronic device of any one of claims 29-34, wherein the electrode includes an opening such that a component can be positioned in the opening and within an outer perimeter of the battery assembly. 37. An electronic device, comprising: an enclosure that includes a bottom wall and sidewall components that combines with the bottom wall to define an internal volume; a battery assembly positioned in the internal volume, the battery assembly having a channel defining a reduced dimension such that the battery assembly 

 includes a first height and a second height, at the channel, that is less than the first height; and a circuit board assembly disposed in the internal volume; an internal component disposed in the internal volume; and a flexible circuit electrically coupled to the circuit board assembly, the flexible circuit passing over the battery assembly along the channel and connecting to the internal component such that the internal component is electrically coupled with the circuit board assembly. 38. The electronic device of claim 37, wherein the battery assembly comprises a first cover element and a second cover element that combines with the first cover element to define to define a housing that includes the channel. 39. The electronic device of claim 38, wherein the battery assembly resembles an L-shape. 40. The electronic device of any one of claims 37-39, further comprising a second component with an outer perimeter of the battery assembly. 41. The electronic device of claim 40, wherein the battery assembly includes an opening, and wherein the second component is positioned in the opening. 42. The electronic device of claim 40, wherein the battery assembly covers the second component and conforms to a shape of the second component. 43. The electronic device of any one of claims 37-39, further comprising: a compartment located in the battery assembly in a location corresponding to the channel; and a circuit board disposed in the compartment, the circuit board configured to monitor energy to the battery assembly. 44. The electronic device of any one of claims 37-39, wherein the circuit board assembly resemble an L-shape. 45. A battery assembly for an electronic device, the battery assembly comprising: a first cover element; a second cover element that combines with the first cover element to define an internal volume; a first electrode positioned in the internal volume; a second electrode positioned in the internal volume and separated from the first electrode by a separator; and 

 a circuit board positioned in the internal volume and configured to monitor energy to the first electrode and the second electrode. 46. The battery assembly of claim 45, wherein the first cover element, the second cover element, the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator are capable of conforming to a shape of an internal component of the electronic device. 47. The battery assembly of claim 45, wherein each of the first electrode and the second electrode resembles a C-shape or an I-shape. 48. The battery assembly of any one of claims 45-47, wherein the first cover element includes a channel, the channel having a first height less than a second height measured from the first cover element to the second cover element. 49. The battery assembly of claim 48, wherein the circuit board is located in a position corresponding to the channel. 50. The battery assembly of any one of claims 45-47, further comprising a through hole defined by an opening in each of the first cover element, the second cover element, the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator. 51. The battery assembly of any one of claims 51, wherein the first cover element and the second cover element resemble an L-shape. 52. The battery assembly of claim 51, wherein the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator resemble the L-shape. 53. A method for assembling a battery assembly for an electronic device, the method comprising: providing a housing that includes a channel defining a reduced dimension in the housing such that the housing includes a first height and a second height, at the channel, that is less than the first height; providing a first electrode and a second electrode, the first electrode and the second electrode configured to generate energy for the battery assembly; providing a separator to include a size and shape in accordance with the first electrode and the second electrode; and inserting the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator into the housing such that the separator is positioned between the first electrode and the second electrode. 54. The method of claim 53, wherein providing the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator comprises die cutting the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator. 55. The method of claim 53, further comprising forming a channel in the housing, the channel configured to receive a flexible circuit. 56. The method of claim 55, further comprising inserting a circuit board into the housing, the circuit board configured to monitor electrical current to the first electrode and the second electrode. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the circuit board disposed in the housing at a location corresponding to the channel. 58. The method of any one of claims 53-57, wherein: the first electrode includes a first opening; the second electrode a second opening, and the separator includes a third opening, wherein the first opening is aligned with the second opening and the third opening. 59. The method of any one of claims 53-57, wherein providing the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator comprises cutting the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator in a shape that resembles an L-shape. 60. The method of any one of claims 53-57, wherein providing the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator comprises cutting the first electrode, the second electrode, and the separator in a shape that resembles an I-shape. 61. A battery assembly carried by an enclosure of a portable electronic device, the enclosure having a rectangular shape, comprising: a battery housing having a first portion extending along a first axis and a second portion extending along a second axis that is perpendicular to the first axis, the housing capable of carrying: a first battery portion including battery cells within the first housing portion, a second battery portion including battery cells within the second housing portion, and an attachment mechanism configured to attach the first battery portion and the second battery portion.
